sanskrit: नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन । न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्िचदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥
hindiTranslation: उस कर्मयोगसे सिद्ध हुए महापुरुषका इस संसारमें न तो कर्म करनेसे कोई प्रयोजन रहता है, और न कर्म न करनेसे ही कोई प्रयोजन रहता है, तथा सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंमें किसी भी प्राणीके साथ इसका किञ्चिन्मात्र भी स्वार्थका सम्बन्ध नहीं रहता ॥
englishTranslation: For the perfected person, no purpose remains in doing, and no purpose remains in not doing. No being in the world is a source of personal gain.
essence: Freedom ends the need to profit from action.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person who has reached inner completeness no longer acts for personal gain. Neither action nor inaction adds anything to that person.
simpleInsight: There is relief in not needing life to pay you back.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and fear. Krishna explains the mature state of action: a person established in inner completeness does not act for reward, nor avoid action for reward. Nothing in the world becomes a bargaining chip.
modernRelevance: You send the message, make the apology, or walk away from the deal without calculating how it will benefit you. The action stands on its own, even when nothing comes back.
translationLiteral: नैव (not at all) / तस्य (for that one) / कृतेन (by doing) / अर्थः (purpose, gain) / न (not) / अकृतेन (by not doing) / इह (here) / कश्चन (any) / न (not) / च (and) / अस्य (for this one) / सर्वभूतेषु (among all beings) / कश्चित् (any) / अर्थव्यपाश्रयः (basis for personal gain)
